properties.name: CAZRI — Central Arid Zone Research Institute

facts: India's premier arid-zone agricultural research institute (ICAR) — founded 1959 at Jodhpur.
Pioneered India's dune-stabilisation science, drip irrigation for arid crops, and khejri (Prosopis cineraria) shelterbelt system.
Regional stations at Bikaner, Pali, Kukma (Kutch) and Leh — spans the entire Thar-Ladakh arid belt.
Developed Rajasthan's foundation seed programme for bajra, guar, moth and cluster-bean.
India's only forestry research institute for the arid zone (ICFRE) — founded 1985.
Develops silvicultural systems for khejri, rohira, kair, ker and neem in <500 mm rainfall.
Runs the National Herbal Garden and the arid-zone tissue-culture bank.
AFRI's shelterbelt models informed India's Green India Mission dryland strategy.

properties.name: CSWRI — Central Sheep & Wool Research Institute

facts: India's flagship sheep-and-wool research institute at Avikanagar (Malpura, Tonk) — founded 1962.
Developed the Avikalin, Bharat Merino and Malpura Cross breeds — India's leading fine-wool synthetics.
Runs the National Wool Research and Training Institute for the northern-region wool cluster.
Rajasthan produces 30% of India's wool — CSWRI's breeding programme underpins that share.
India's National Research Centre on Camel — Bikaner (Jhorbir), founded 1984 as a project directorate, upgraded to NRC in 1995.
Home to Asia's first camel-milk dairy — commercialising camel milk since 2015.
Maintains the germplasm of India's four camel breeds: Bikaneri, Jaisalmeri, Kachhi, Mewari.
Rajasthan holds ~41% of India's camel population; NRC-Camel underpins the Rebari pastoralist economy.
Rajasthan's only AIIMS — one of six second-generation AIIMS opened 2012 under PMSSY.
Announced 2004 by Vajpayee, operationalised 2012 with 960-bed hospital.
Anchors Rajasthan's western-tertiary healthcare — covers Barmer, Jaisalmer, Bikaner catchment.
Runs India's first Fibrodysplasia Ossificans Progressiva (FOP) research programme.
Rajasthan's only IIT — established 2008 at Karwar (Nagaur road, Jodhpur).
Distinctive academic programme: no rigid department boundaries; interdisciplinary M-schools model.
Anchors the Jodhpur AI and IoT cluster; hosts India's first drone-technology research lab (2021).
The IIT campus is one of India's most sustainable — 100% solar-powered daytime operations.
Rajasthan's only IIM — established 2011 as one of six new-generation IIMs.
One of India's fastest-rising business schools; anchors the western-India MBA market.
The Balicha campus is India's second-largest IIM campus by area (300+ acres).
Home to India's only IIM PhD programme with a formal Digital Enterprise Management stream.
One of India's premier National Law Universities — established 1999 by the Rajasthan State Act.
First NLU in India to offer a two-year LLM programme (2006) and integrated BA-BSc-LLB (2004).
Located at Mandore (Jodhpur) — the ancient Marwar capital.
Consistently ranks in India's top-5 law schools.
Founded 1929 by Ghanshyam Das Birla as Birla College — India's first industrialist-funded science institute.
Achieved deemed-university status in 1964 as Birla Institute of Technology and Science.
Runs India's largest industry-linked engineering internship programme (BITS Practice School).
Anchors the Shekhawati engineering-talent belt; alumni founded Cognizant, Tata Consultancy, Flipkart.
Rajasthan's first Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) — established 1976 at Fatehpur Shekhawati.
Pioneered India's district-level KVK extension model, later scaled to 700+ KVKs across the country.
Anchor of the Shekhawati groundnut and bajra research cluster.
Directorate of Rapeseed-Mustard Research (SEVAR) at Sewar (Bharatpur), founded 1993.
India's premier mustard breeding institute; Bharatpur district is India's largest mustard producer.
Developed the RH-30, RH-819, Vardaan and Bio-902 varieties that anchor Rajasthan's rabi mustard belt.
Rajasthan produces 45% of India's mustard — SEVAR underpins that share.
Twin arid-fruit research centres at Bikaner — Ber (Indian jujube) and Khajur (date palm), both founded 1978.
Developed arid-zone commercial cultivars: Gola, Mundia, Kaithli (ber); Barhee, Halawy, Khadrawy (date).
Rajasthan produces India's largest ber crop; date palm cultivation has expanded 4x since 2000 thanks to KVK extension.
First institution to formally research indigenous Indian cattle breeds and Panchagavya products.
Pathmeda (Sanchore, Jalore) is home to India's largest gaushala — over 200,000 cattle sheltered.
Sanchore also hosts India's first Gau-Mutra Bank.

properties.name: Manikya Lal Verma Adivasi Research & Training Institute

facts: Rajasthan's premier tribal research institute — established at Udaipur in 1964.
Anchors research on Bhil, Meena, Garasia, Damor, Sahariya and Kathodia tribes.
Publishes the Adivasi Sansar journal and runs the state's tribal-language documentation programme.
Named after Rajasthan's tribal-rights pioneer Manikya Lal Verma (1897-1969).
Rajasthan's state archives — established at Jaipur in 1955, shifted to Bikaner in 1966.
Holds Rajasthan's largest Persian, Urdu and Rajasthani manuscript collection.
Houses Mughal farmans, princely-state records, and hand-painted Rajput miniatures.
One of India's largest state-level archival collections — over 8 lakh documents.
Founded 1960 by Padma Shri Komal Kothari at Borunda (Bilada, Jodhpur).
India's premier ethnomusicology and folk-arts research centre — Rupayan documented 1000+ Langa-Manganiar songs.
Its work led to UNESCO recognition of Rajasthan's oral folk traditions.
Continues to run Rajasthan's largest folk-art field research programme.
Rajasthan's oldest folk-arts research centre — founded 22 February 1952 by Padma Shri Devi Lal Samar.
Houses the world's largest collection of Rajasthani puppetry (Kathputli).
Runs Rajasthan's only professional puppetry academy; the 'Puppet Museum' is a UNESCO study centre.
Anchors Udaipur's cultural-heritage tourism circuit.
Founded 1931 by Mohan Singh Mehta as Rajasthan's first modern educational society under Mewar princely rule.
Runs the state's oldest teacher-training institute (VBTC) and India's largest rural-college network under a single society.
Anchors the Aravalli-region rural education movement; pioneered vocational Krishak Vidyapeeth model in the 1950s.
